title: Scite-grounded Citicoline enrichment pilot
review_notes: This proposal does not alter evidence_tier, evidence_grade, profile_status, or publishing controls.
The dosage field describes specific regimens reported in human cognition studies and is not individualized advice.
Positive memory findings are framed as preliminary and population-specific because study populations, doses, outcomes, and designs are heterogeneous.
The 12-week 500 mg/day trial used a branded citicoline ingredient; avoid generalizing one product-specific trial to all products.
Parser-supported evidence_rationale and trial_design_insight fields were intentionally omitted because those columns are not present in the current Entity_Master schema.

sources.title: Citicoline and Memory Function in Healthy Older Adults: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
Is Citicoline Effective in Preventing and Slowing Down Dementia?—A Systematic Review and a Meta-Analysis
Citicoline Efficiency on Cognitive Function: A Systematic Review
sources.authors: Cano-Cuenca, Solís-García del Pozo, and Jordán
sources.journal: Journal of Aging Research and Lifestyle

sources.title: Citicoline: A Superior Form of Choline?

changes.expected_old_value: Citicoline (CDP-choline) is a choline source studied as non-stimulant support for attention and focus, and for cognition in older adults. Human evidence is limited and the effects are modest — it is a supportive option rather than a dramatic cognitive enhancer, and works best alongside sleep and healthy habits.
changes.new_value: Citicoline (CDP-choline) supplies choline and cytidine-derived precursors used in phosphatidylcholine metabolism. Small randomized trials in selected older adults suggest possible short-term improvements in memory, including a 12-week study using 500 mg/day, but the broader evidence is heterogeneous and does not establish a large or universal cognitive-enhancing effect.
changes.rationale: Adds mechanism context, a specific human-trial anchor, and explicit limits against overgeneralizing heterogeneous cognition evidence while avoiding a broad tolerability claim not quantified in the retrieved excerpts.

changes.new_value: Human cognition studies cited here used 500 mg/day for 12 weeks and 1,000 mg/day for 28 days to 3 months. These are study regimens, not individualized dosing advice.
changes.rationale: Replaces a generalized dose range with exact regimens reported in the retrieved human cognition literature.
